definition: A critical conceptual unit within the top term domain of the Exegesis. Mentions: 92.
interpretive_note: Borrowed from Hinduism, Maya is the veil of illusion that conceals the true nature of reality (Brahman). In the Exegesis, Maya is synonymous with the Black Iron Prison—it is the spurious, holographic projection of space and time generated by the Demiurge. Dick believed that our everyday perception is essentially a hallucination designed to keep us entrapped in a state of spiritual amnesia. To pierce the veil of Maya is to recognize that empirical reality is not solid, but rather a sequence of information states, allowing the individual to perceive the underlying, eternal, and rational structure of the cosmic mind.

To pierce the veil of Maya is to recognize that empirical reality is not solid, but rather a sequence of information states, allowing the individual to perceive the underlying, eternal, and rational structure of the cosmic mind.
full_description:
Borrowed from Hinduism, Maya is the veil of illusion that conceals the true nature of reality (Brahman). In the Exegesis, Maya is synonymous with the Black Iron Prison—it is the spurious, holographic projection of space and time generated by the Demiurge. Dick believed that our everyday perception is essentially a hallucination designed to keep us entrapped in a state of spiritual amnesia. To pierce the veil of Maya is to recognize that empirical reality is not solid, but rather a sequence of information states, allowing the individual to perceive the underlying, eternal, and rational structure of the cosmic mind.

**Category:** Top Term

**Frequency:** 92 mentions.

**From the Exegesis:**
> This is what I saw; what I saw rolled back was the "snow" of that long winter. Back to Spring. These are the dust, too, the dust and snow: dust in summer, snow in winter, but there was the Ggod only sleeping, as if dead .

I saw that it can melt away in an instant, and reveal the original "season...
> Our reality can be manipulated retroactively; that is, our past can be rewoven, and without hesitation, as an automatic process, our several memory-systems will fill in the blanks and make smooth the reweaving (i.e. we won't realize that alterations in our lineal-time past took place).

A certain...
> A certain portion of the phenomenal world which we encounter is hologram-like, projected and false, to fill in missing spaces, as in the time extension, missing elements are filling in along similar lines. Therefore we see total continuity in both extensions, time and space, without awareness of ...

**Related terms:** Acts (related), Advent (related), After (related), Again (related), Although (related), Anamnesis (related)

I saw that it can melt away in an instant, and reveal the original "season" or being or reality.

A certain portion of the phenomenal world which we encounter is hologram-like, projected and false, to fill in missing spaces, as in the time extension, missing elements are filling in along similar lines. Therefore we see total continuity in both extensions, time and space, without awareness of dysfunction or rupture. The mirror opposite universe. It is not --repeat NOT-- another universe, but this one reflected. Thus as Parmenides said, Form Two is actually Form One (reflected, or seen in reverse, or seen backward or upside down or from outside). We have universe and -- "as seen by reflection in the bottom of a polished pan.) The most important realization of Parmenides: Yin is (or becomes) Yang.
